title: satureja subnuda
intro: Satureja subnuda, also known as the desert savory, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a shrubby herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its small, white or pink flowers and its distinctive fragrance. Satureja subnuda has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Satureja subnuda, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, can be used to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
